risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

sect1.title: Chapter 17

listitem.para: Positive pressure ventilation, delivered by any means (resuscitation bag or T-piece resuscitator), and/or cardiac compressions are required for 99465.
True. Only 1 initial daily inpatient critical care code (99468, 99471, 99475) is reported per calendar day and per hospital stay for a given patient.

sect1.para: 3. b. Report only initial or subsequent critical care (99468–99469), according to whether this is the first or subsequent episode of neonatal critical care during the same hospital stay when the neonate becomes critically ill on a day when initial or subsequent intensive care services (99477–99480) have been provided by the same physician(s) or QHP(s) from the same group practice.
4. b. Subsequent intensive care services (99478–99480) are differentiated by the infant's weight in grams on the date of service.
5. b. Codes 33946–33949 include managing the ECMO or ECLS circuit and related patient issues.
